commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Gates, where sentence was usually passed. (Haydock)
The Jews had judges at Babylon, (Daniel xiii. 5.) but not at first, nor everywhere.

commentaries.author: Thomas Aquinas
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh The elders have ceased from the gate, the young men from their music. Here misery is considered in relation to the people's lost possessions. First, as persons' offices as stated: "The old men have quit the city gate. " Namely, exercising their office at the city gate. Then: "the young men their music": That is, as exercising their office as leaders in chorusing. As I Maccabees 2:9 refers: Her babes have been killed in her streets, her youths by the sword of the foe.
